title: hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. longistipes
intro: Hieracium scorzonerifolium subsp. longistipes, also known as Long-stiped Hawk's Beard, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots are used to prepare herbal remedies, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to support the immune system, reduce stress, and promote overall well-being. Long-stiped Hawk's Beard is also valued for its potential to stimulate digestion, reduce bloating, and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome.
